title: Building Your First RAG Pipeline
subtitle: RAG & Vector Search — Like One Academy

sections.text: You understand embeddings. You understand vector search. Now we are going to build something real — a RAG pipeline that turns a simple question into an intelligent, memory-aware answer.

steps.label: Store
steps.description: Embed your documents and save them to a vector database

steps.label: Retrieve
steps.description: Search for the most relevant documents given a question

steps.label: Augment
steps.description: Inject those documents into your AI prompt

steps.label: Generate
steps.description: Let the AI answer using both the question and the context

sections.text: Step one is storage. You take your documents — notes, articles, memories, whatever matters — and convert each one into an embedding. Then you store both the text and the embedding in your database. pgvector makes this a single table.

sections.label: Store Embeddings

sections.text: Step two is retrieval. When a question comes in, you embed the question using the same model, then use pgvector to find the closest stored memories. The match operator orders results by cosine distance.

sections.label: Search by Similarity
sections.code:
-- Find the 5 most relevant memories
select content,
  1 - (embedding <=> $question_embedding) as similarity
from memories
order by embedding <=> $question_embedding
limit 5;

sections.text: Step three is augmentation. You take those retrieved memories and inject them directly into the prompt you send to the AI. This is the augment in retrieval augmented generation. The AI now has context it would not have had otherwise.

sections.label: Augment the Prompt

sections.text: Step four is generation. The AI reads your question plus the retrieved context and gives you a grounded, accurate answer. No hallucination. No guessing. Just your knowledge, amplified by intelligence.

sections.quote: Memory is the diary we all carry about with us.

sections.heading: Like One Academy
sections.subtext: You just built a RAG pipeline. Now go deeper.
